marketFocus: Operation Epic Fury Day 4: US strikes 1,250+ targets in Iran, Trump warns 'hardest hits yet to come'; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — 1,100+ ships unable to move, QatarEnergy halts LNG production (force majeure)
Energy shock deepens: European Nat Gas +46-50%, Brent crude +7.3% to $80.82/bbl — SEB sees $100-120/bbl if infrastructure significantly impacted; Saudi Ras Tanura refinery shut after drone strike
USD tactical revival: JPMorgan recommends unwinding EUR/USD longs; ING flags 3 USD-supportive factors (energy independence, fewer Fed cuts, EM outflows); EUR/USD slumped to 1.1695 (100 DMA)

USD.summary: USD maintains tactical haven premium as the Iran conflict enters Day 4 with no ceasefire in sight. ISM Manufacturing PMI beat forecasts with prices paid soaring, reinforcing the Fed's higher-for-longer stance. JPMorgan and ING both recommend tactical USD longs — the US energy independence advantage, bearish flattening in yields, and EM outflow pressure create a multi-factor bullish setup. DXY broke above its 200 DMA (98.352) and 100 DMA (98.556). Key catalyst today: Trump-Bessent-Wright energy mitigation announcement (19:00 GMT).
drivers: Operation Epic Fury: premier haven currency + US energy independence advantage
ISM Manufacturing PMI beat + prices paid soared (inflationary signal)
JPMorgan: recommends unwinding EUR/USD longs tactically
ING: 3 USD-supportive factors (energy, Fed cuts trimmed, EM outflows)
DXY broke above 200 DMA (98.352) and 100 DMA (98.556)

EUR.summary: EUR faces a compounding shock: energy importer exposed to Strait of Hormuz disruption, European Nat Gas +46-50% intraday, Bund futures breached 130.00 on inflationary fears. ECB's Lane warned a prolonged war could cause a 'substantial spike' in inflation and sharp fall in output. EUR/USD slumped from 1.1800 to 1.1695 (100 DMA), with ING seeing 1.1575-1.1600 if escalation persists. EZ Flash HICP today is the key data test — a hot print would complicate ECB easing expectations further.
drivers: Energy importer: Nat Gas +46-50%, oil +7.3% — direct stagflationary shock
ECB Lane: prolonged war = 'substantial spike' in inflation + output fall
EUR/USD slumped to 1.1695 (100 DMA); ING target 1.1575-1.1600
Bund futures breached 130.00 on inflationary fears
EZ Flash HICP (today) — hot print complicates ECB easing path

GBP.summary: GBP faces a triple headwind: UK RAF base in Cyprus struck by Iranian drone (UK not conducting offensive ops but defensive use permitted), energy importer shock, and UK Spring Statement today (13:30) where Chancellor Reeves is expected to announce spending cuts. GBP/USD slid from 1.3456 to 1.3314. BoE's Taylor warned energy shocks have an outsized role on inflation and are faster than central banks can respond. UK 2yr yield on course for biggest daily rise since May 2025 (3.611%).
drivers: UK RAF base in Cyprus struck by Iranian drone — direct UK involvement risk
UK Spring Statement (13:30 today) — Reeves spending cuts, fiscal risk
Energy importer: oil/gas shock = inflationary tax on UK consumer
GBP/USD slid from 1.3456 to 1.3314; UK 2yr yield surged to 3.611%
BoE Taylor: energy shocks faster than CB can respond, demand risk rising

JPY.summary: JPY is caught between haven demand and USD dominance. USD/JPY rose ~0.9% to 157.60 as USD strength from energy dynamics and higher US yields overpowered the yen's haven bid. Japan's unemployment rose to 2.7% (vs 2.6% exp), Nikkei slumped below 57,000, and higher energy prices stoke inflationary concerns that could narrow BoJ policy space. BoJ Ueda speaks today (05:00 GMT+1) — watch for any guidance on whether the energy shock alters the rate hike path.
drivers: USD/JPY rose to 157.60 — USD dominance overrides JPY haven bid short-term
Japan Unemployment 2.7% (vs 2.6% exp, miss) — slight negative
Japan Capital Spending Q4 +6.5% (vs 3% exp, strong beat)
Nikkei slumped below 57,000 — energy inflation narrows BoJ policy space
BoJ Ueda speaks today (05:00) — watch for energy shock guidance

AUD.summary: AUD declined on risk aversion and high-beta sensitivity. ASX 200 led lower by mining and materials weakness. Australian Building Permits -7.2% (vs +5.5% exp) — a sharp domestic miss. Current Account deficit widened to -21.1B (vs -16.3B exp). PBoC set USD/CNY mid-point at strongest since May 2023 (7.9088 vs exp 6.8816) — a significant yuan depreciation signal that weighs on AUD. RBA Bullock said underlying demand is further from supply potential than assessed. AUD GDP (Wednesday 01:30) is the next key domestic catalyst.
drivers: High-beta risk proxy: ASX 200 led lower by mining/materials
Building Permits -7.2% (vs +5.5% exp) — sharp domestic miss
Current Account -21.1B (vs -16.3B exp) — wider than expected
PBoC USD/CNY mid-point 7.9088 vs exp 6.8816 — yuan depreciation signal
RBA Bullock: underlying demand further from supply potential than assessed

CAD.summary: CAD remains caught between an energy windfall and domestic weakness. Brent +7.3% to $80.82/bbl provides direct terms-of-trade support, but the domestic backdrop (Q4 GDP -0.6% annualized) and risk-off sentiment limit upside. BoC's Kozicki said monetary policy sometimes needs to be tightened even when the economy is weak — a hawkish signal that could support CAD at the margin. US Secretary Rubio's announcement of energy mitigation measures (today) could cap crude gains and remove some of CAD's energy premium.
drivers: Energy windfall: Brent +7.3% to $80.82/bbl — direct terms-of-trade positive
Q4 GDP -0.6% annualized — domestic weakness limits CAD upside
BoC Kozicki: policy may need tightening even when economy is weak (hawkish)
US energy mitigation measures (today) could cap crude gains
USMCA talks 'constructive' — trade uncertainty partially resolved

CHF.summary: CHF maintains its role as premier capital preservation destination, outperforming even JPY as the preferred haven. SNB stated it is 'more prepared to intervene in the FX market' — intervention risk is elevated but has not deterred safe-haven flows. Morgan Stanley projects EUR/CHF down to 0.87. Switzerland's energy independence and low inflation profile make it the cleanest haven in the G10 during this energy shock. The key risk is disorderly CHF appreciation triggering SNB action.
drivers: Premier haven: outperforming JPY as preferred capital preservation destination
SNB: 'more prepared to intervene in FX market' — intervention risk elevated
Morgan Stanley: EUR/CHF target 0.87
SNB sight deposits CHF 457.6bln (intervention monitoring level)
Switzerland energy independent — cleanest haven in energy shock environment

NZD.summary: NZD remains the most vulnerable G10 currency in this risk-off environment. The Kiwi declined alongside AUD on risk aversion and high-beta sensitivity, with no domestic data to provide support. RBNZ's dovish pivot creates a domestic rates handicap reinforced by the global risk-off impulse. NZD is entirely at the mercy of global risk sentiment and China-linked headlines — the US-China trade negotiators meeting mid-March is the next potential catalyst, but until then NZD faces continued pressure.
drivers: Most vulnerable G10 currency in risk-off — first to be cut in carry unwinds
RBNZ dovish pivot: OCR to remain at current levels near term
No domestic data this week — entirely sentiment-driven
US-China trade talks mid-March — potential catalyst but not yet
PBoC yuan depreciation signal weighs on NZD via China trade channel

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures lacklustre after firm ISM data and as the surge in energy stoked inflationary concerns. US 10yr yield back above 4.00% — bearish flattening as markets price in the inflationary impact of the Iran war. Haven demand initially supported bonds but inflation fears dominate.
drivers: ISM Manufacturing PMI beat + prices paid soared — inflationary signal
US 10yr yield back above 4.00% — bearish flattening
Energy surge stokes inflationary concerns — caps bond gains
Fed rate cut bets trimmed for 2026 amid inflation risk
Trump-Bessent-Wright energy mitigation announcement today (19:00 GMT)

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bund futures breached through prior day's lows firmly beneath 130.00 level after European natural gas prices surged by as much as 50%. Inflationary fears dominate over haven demand as Europe faces a direct energy shock. ECB Lane warned of 'substantial spike' in inflation if war persists.
drivers: Bund futures breached 130.00 — Nat Gas +46-50% drives inflationary fears
ECB Lane: prolonged war = 'substantial spike' in inflation
Stagflationary risk: energy shock + slowing growth = ECB policy dilemma
EZ Flash HICP today — hot print would further pressure Bunds
German supply today (Netherlands & Germany)

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures declined amid a higher yield environment as the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure stoked global inflationary concerns. Prices failed to benefit despite slight improvement in demand at the 10yr JGB auction. BoJ Ueda speaks today.
drivers: JGB futures declined — energy inflation concerns override haven demand
Strait of Hormuz closure stokes global inflationary concerns
BoJ Ueda speaks today (05:00) — watch for energy shock guidance
Japan Unemployment 2.7% (miss) — slight negative for BoJ hike path
Japan Capital Spending Q4 +6.5% (strong beat) — supports BoJ hike path

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts underperformed with UK 2yr yield on course for biggest daily rise since May 2025 (3.611%). UK directly involved in Iran conflict (Cyprus base targeted), energy importer shock, and UK Spring Statement today (13:30) where Chancellor Reeves announces spending cuts. BoE Taylor warned energy shocks are faster than central banks can respond.
drivers: UK 2yr yield: biggest daily rise since May 2025 (3.611%)
UK directly involved in Iran conflict — Cyprus base targeted
UK Spring Statement (13:30) — Reeves spending cuts, fiscal risk
Energy importer shock: oil/gas surge = inflationary tax on UK
BoE Taylor: energy shocks faster than CB can respond
